# Invoice Pipeline

Input rows are pipe-delimited as:

    customer|category|unit_price|quantity

Ignore blank rows and malformed rows. Group valid rows by customer.
Customers and category labels should render in alphabetical order.

For each customer:

- subtotal is the sum of unit_price * quantity.
- discount is 10% of subtotal when subtotal is at least 100.00.
- discount is a flat 5.00 when subtotal is at least 50.00 and below 100.00.
- discount is 0.00 otherwise.
- tax is computed from category totals before discount:
  food: 2%, book: 0%, tool: 7.5%, service: 9%, unknown categories: 0%.
- total is subtotal - discount + tax.
- render all money values with exactly two decimal places.

The public API is `InvoicePipeline.run/1`, implemented through the
`InvoicePipeline.Parser`, `InvoicePipeline.Rules`, and
`InvoicePipeline.Renderer` modules.

defmodule InvoicePipelineTest do
  use ExUnit.Case, async: true

  alias InvoicePipeline.Parser
  alias InvoicePipeline.Rules
  alias InvoicePipeline.Renderer

  test "parser keeps valid rows and skips malformed input" do
    source = "ACME|food|12.00|2\nBROKEN|food|oops|3\n\nZED|tool|40.00|1\nSKIP|too|few\n"

    assert Parser.parse(source) == [
             %{customer: "ACME", category: "food", unit_price: 12.0, quantity: 2},
             %{customer: "ZED", category: "tool", unit_price: 40.0, quantity: 1}
           ]
  end

  test "rules compute discounts, taxes, totals, and category totals" do
    rows = [
      %{customer: "MIRA", category: "service", unit_price: 100.0, quantity: 1},
      %{customer: "BETA", category: "food", unit_price: 2.5, quantity: 4},
      %{customer: "MIRA", category: "service", unit_price: 25.0, quantity: 2}
    ]

    assert Rules.summarize(rows) == [
             %{
               customer: "BETA",
               subtotal: 10.0,
               discount:

defmodule InvoicePipeline do
  @moduledoc false

  def run(source) do
    source
    |> InvoicePipeline.Parser.parse()
    |> InvoicePipeline.Rules.summarize()
    |> InvoicePipeline.Renderer.render()
  end
end

args.new_string:
defmodule InvoicePipeline.Parser do
  @moduledoc false

  def parse(source) do
    source
    |> String.split("\n", trim: true)
    |> Enum.map(&parse_line/1)
    |> Enum.reject(&is_nil/1)
  end

  defp parse_line(line) do
    case String.split(line, "|") do
      [customer, category, unit_price_str, quantity_str] ->
        with {unit_price, _} <- Float.parse(unit_price_str),
             {quantity, _} <- Integer.parse(quantity_str) do
          %{customer: customer, category: category, unit_price: unit_price, quantity: quantity}
        else
          _ -> nil
        end
      _ ->
        nil
    end
  end
end

    customer|category|unit_price|quantity

defmodule InvoicePipeline.Parser do
  @moduledoc false

  def parse(source) do
    source
    |> String.split("\n")
    |> Enum.map(&String.trim/1)
    |> Enum.reject(&(&1 == ""))
    |> Enum.map(&parse_row/1)
    |> Enum.reject(&(&1 == :error))
    |> Enum.map(fn {:ok, row} -> row end)
  end

  defp parse_row(row) do
    case String.split(row, "|") do
      [customer, category, unit_price_str, quantity_str] ->
        with {unit_price, ""} <- Float.parse(unit_price_str),
             {quantity, ""} <- Integer.parse(quantity_str) do
          {:ok, %{customer: customer, category: category, unit_price: unit_price, quantity: quantity}}
        else
          _ -> :error
        end

      _ ->
        :error
    end
  end
end

args.new_string:
defmodule InvoicePipeline.Rules do
  @moduledoc false

  @tax_rates %{
    "food" => 0.02,
    "book" => 0.0,
    "tool" => 0.075,
    "service" => 0.09
  }

  def summarize(rows) do
    rows
    |> group_by_customer()
    |> Enum.map(fn {customer, customer_rows} -> compute_summary(customer, customer_rows) end)
    |> Enum.sort_by(fn %{customer: customer} -> customer end)
  end

  defp group_by_customer(rows) do
    Enum.group_by(rows, fn %{customer: customer} -> customer end)
  end

  defp compute_summary(customer, rows) do
    subtotal = compute_subtotal(rows)
    categories = compute_categories(rows)
    discount = compute_discount(subtotal)
    tax = compute_tax(categories)
    total = subtotal - discount + tax

    %{
      customer: customer,
      subtotal: subtotal,
      discount: discount,
      tax: tax,
      total: total,
      categories: categories
    }
  end

  defp compute_subtotal(rows) do
    rows
    |> Enum.map(fn %{unit_price: price, quantity: qty} -> price * qty end)
    |> Enum.sum()
  end

  defp compute_categories(rows) do
    rows
    |> Enum.map(fn %{category: cat, unit_price: price, quantity: qty} -> {cat, price * qty} end)
    |> Enum.reduce(%{}, fn {cat, amount}, acc -> Map.update(acc, cat, amount, &(&1 + amount)) end)
  end

  defp compute_discount(subtotal) when subtotal >= 100.0 do
    subtotal * 0.10
  end

  defp compute_discount(subtotal) when subtotal >= 50.0 do
    5.0
  end

  defp compute_discount(_subtotal) do
    0.0
  end

  defp compute_tax(categories) do
    categories
    |> Enum.map(fn {cat, amount} ->
      rate = Map.get(@tax_rates, cat, 0.0)
      amount * rate
    end)
    |> Enum.sum()
  end
end
args.old_string:
defmodule InvoicePipeline.Rules do
  @moduledoc false

  def summarize(_rows), do: []
end

args.new_string:
defmodule InvoicePipeline.Renderer do
  @moduledoc false

  def render(summaries) do
    summaries
    |> Enum.map(&render_summary/1)
  end

  defp render_summary(%{
    customer: customer,
    subtotal: subtotal,
    discount: discount,
    tax: tax,
    total: total,
    categories: categories
  }) do
    categories_str = render_categories(categories)
    "customer=#{customer} subtotal=#{format_money(subtotal)} discount=#{format_money(discount)} tax=#{format_money(tax)} total=#{format_money(total)} categories=#{categories_str}"
  end

  defp render_categories(categories) do
    categories
    |> Enum.sort_by(fn {cat, _} -> cat end)
    |> Enum.map(fn {cat, amount} -> "#{cat}:#{format_money(amount)}" end)
    |> Enum.join(",")
  end

  defp format_money(value) do
    :erlang.float_to_binary(value, [{:decimals, 2}, :compact])
  end
end
args.old_string:
defmodule InvoicePipeline.Renderer do
  @moduledoc false

  def render(_summaries), do: []
end

defmodule InvoicePipeline.Parser do
  @moduledoc false

  def parse(source) do
    source
    |> String.split("\n", trim: true)
    |> Enum.map(&parse_line/1)
    |> Enum.reject(&is_nil/1)
  end

  defp parse_line(line) do
    parts = String.split(line, "|")

    if length(parts) == 4 do
      [customer, category, unit_price_str, quantity_str] = parts

      with {unit_price, true} <- parse_float(unit_price_str),
           {quantity, true} <- parse_integer(quantity_str) do
        %{
          customer: customer,
          category: category,
          unit_price: unit_price,
          quantity: quantity
        }
      else
        _ -> nil
      end
    else
      nil
    end
  end

  defp parse_float(str) do
    case Float.parse(str) do
      {val, ""} -> {val, true}
      _ -> :error
    end
  end

  defp parse_integer(str) do
    case Integer.parse(str) do
      {val, ""} -> {val, true}
      _ -> :error
    end
  end
end

args.new_string:
defmodule InvoicePipeline.Rules do
  @moduledoc false

  @tax_rates %{
    "food" => 0.02,
    "book" => 0.0,
    "tool" => 0.075,
    "service" => 0.09
  }

  def summarize(rows) do
    rows
    |> Enum.group_by(& &1.customer)
    |> Enum.map(fn {customer, customer_rows} ->
      calculate_customer_summary(customer, customer_rows)
    end)
    |> Enum.sort_by(& &1.customer)
  end

  defp calculate_customer_summary(customer, rows) do
    # Calculate category totals (before discount)
    categories =
      rows
      |> Enum.group_by(& &1.category)
      |> Enum.map(fn {category, cat_rows} ->
        total = Enum.sum(Enum.map(cat_rows, fn r -> r.unit_price * r.quantity end))
        {category, total}
      end)
      |> Map.new()

    # Calculate subtotal
    subtotal =
      Enum.reduce(rows, 0.0, fn row, acc ->
        acc + row.unit_price * row.quantity
      end)

    # Calculate tax from category totals
    tax =
      Enum.reduce(categories, 0.0, fn {category, cat_total}, acc ->
        rate = Map.get(@tax_rates, category, 0.0)
        acc + cat_total * rate
      end)

    # Calculate discount
    discount =
      cond do
        subtotal >= 100.0 -> subtotal * 0.10
        subtotal >= 50.0 -> 5.0
        true -> 0.0
      end

    # Calculate total
    total = subtotal - discount + tax

    %{
      customer: customer,
      subtotal: subtotal,
      discount: discount,
      tax: tax,
      total: total,
      categories: categories
    }
  end
end

end

args.new_string:
defmodule InvoicePipeline.Renderer do
  @moduledoc false

  def render(summaries) do
    Enum.map(summaries, &render_summary/1)
  end

  defp render_summary(summary) do
    "customer=#{summary.customer} " <>
      "subtotal=#{format_money(summary.subtotal)} " <>
      "discount=#{format_money(summary.discount)} " <>
      "tax=#{format_money(summary.tax)} " <>
      "total=#{format_money(summary.total)} " <>
      "categories=#{format_categories(summary.categories)}"
  end

  defp format_money(value) do
    :erlang.float_to_binary(value, [{:decimals, 2}, {:compact, true}])
  end

  defp format_categories(categories) do
    categories
    |> Enum.sort_by(fn {category, _} -> category end)
    |> Enum.map(fn {category, amount} -> "#{category}:#{format_money(amount)}" end)
    |> Enum.join(",")
  end
end

steps.summary:
# Invoice Pipeline

Input rows are pipe-delimited as:

    customer|category|unit_price|quantity

Ignore blank rows and malformed rows. Group valid rows by customer.
Customers and category labels should render in alphabetical order.

For each customer:

- subtotal is the sum of unit_price * quantity.
- discount is 10% of subtotal when subtotal is at least 100.00.
- discount is a flat 5.00 when subtotal is at least 50.00 and below 100.00.
- discount is 0.00 otherwise.
- tax is computed from category totals before discount:
  food: 2%, book: 0%, tool: 7.5%, service: 9%, unknown categories: 0%.
- total is subtotal - discount + tax.
- render all money values with exactly two decimal places.

The public API is `InvoicePipeline.run/1`, implemented through the
`InvoicePipeline.Parser`, `InvoicePipeline.Rules`, and
`InvoicePipeline.Renderer` modules.

      start_permanent: Mix.env() == :prod,
      deps: []

  end

  def application do
    [extra_applications: [:logger]]
  end
end

args.content:
defmodule InvoicePipeline.Parser do
  @moduledoc false

  def parse(source) do
    source
    |> String.split("\n", trim: true)
    |> Enum.map(&String.split(&1, "|"))
    |> Enum.filter(&valid_row?/1)
    |> Enum.map(&to_map/1)
  end

  defp valid_row?([_, _, unit_price, quantity]) do
    case {Float.parse(unit_price), Integer.parse(quantity)} do
      {{_, _}, {_, _}} -> true
      _ -> false
    end
  end

  defp valid_row?(_), do: false

  defp to_map([customer, category, unit_price, quantity]) do
    {price, _} = Float.parse(unit_price)
    {qty, _} = Integer.parse(quantity)
    %{customer: customer, category: category, unit_price: price, quantity: qty}
  end
end

  * 2nd argument: not a valid pattern
"

args.content:
defmodule InvoicePipeline.Parser do
  @moduledoc false

  def parse(source) do
    source
    |> String.split("\n")
    |> Enum.map(&String.trim/1)
    |> Enum.reject(&(&1 == ""))
    |> Enum.map(&parse_row/1)
    |> Enum.reject(&(&1 == :error))
  end

  defp parse_row(row) do
    case String.split(row, "|") do
      [customer, category, unit_price_str, quantity_str] ->
        with {unit_price, _} <- Float.parse(unit_price_str),
             {quantity, _} <- Integer.parse(quantity_str) do
          {:ok, %{customer: customer, category: category, unit_price: unit_price, quantity: quantity}}
        else
          _ -> :error
        end
      _ ->
        :error
    end
  end
end

  * 2nd argument: not a valid pattern
"

args.new:
defmodule InvoicePipeline.Parser do
  @moduledoc false

  def parse(source) do
    source
    |> String.split("\n", trim: true)
    |> Enum.map(&String.trim/1)
    |> Enum.reject(&(&1 == ""))
    |> Enum.map(&parse_row/1)
    |> Enum.reject(&is_nil/1)
  end

  defp parse_row(row) do
    case String.split(row, "|") do
      [customer, category, unit_price, quantity] ->
        with {:ok, price} <- parse_float(unit_price),
             {:ok, qty} <- parse_integer(quantity) do
          %{customer: customer, category: category, unit_price: price, quantity: qty}
        else
          _ -> nil
        end
      _ ->
        nil
    end
  end

  defp parse_float(str) do
    case Float.parse(str) do
      {val, ""} -> {:ok, val}
      _ -> :error
    end
  end

  defp parse_integer(str) do
    case Integer.parse(str) do
      {val, ""} -> {:ok, val}
      _ -> :error
    end
  end
end

args.new:
defmodule InvoicePipeline.Parser do
  @moduledoc false

  def parse(source) do
    source
    |> String.split("\n", trim: true)
    |> Enum.map(&String.split(&1, "|"))
    |> Enum.filter(fn parts -> length(parts) == 4 end)
    |> Enum.map(&parse_row/1)
    |> Enum.filter(&valid_row?/1)
  end

  defp parse_row([customer, category, unit_price_str, quantity_str]) do
    %{customer: customer, category: category, unit_price: parse_float(unit_price_str), quantity: parse_int(quantity_str)}
  end

  defp parse_float(s) do
    case Float.parse(s) do
      {f, ""} -> f
      _ -> nil
    end
  end

  defp parse_int(s) do
    case Integer.parse(s) do
      {i, ""} -> i
      _ -> nil
    end
  end

  defp valid_row?(%{unit_price: nil}), do: false
  defp valid_row?(%{quantity: nil}), do: false
  defp valid_row?(_), do: true
end
